nav.footerTranslate: Help us translate or improve OcerDNS in your language. Learn more

home.title: OcerDNS
home.subtitle: Intelligent DNS filtering, security protection, and privacy management for your home network.

features.dnsFilteringDesc: Block malicious domains, ads, and trackers with intelligent filtering rules.
features.securityDesc: Real-time threat detection and blocking against phishing and malware domains.
features.privacyDesc: Prevent DNS leaks and protect your browsing privacy from prying eyes.
features.multiDeviceDesc: Apply profiles across all your devices with centralized management.

home.featuresTitle: Everything you need to control DNS traffic
home.featuresDesc: One platform for personal privacy, family protection, and team-level DNS security.
home.f1Desc: Block phishing, malware, botnet, ransomware, and suspicious domains before connections happen.
home.f2Desc: Stop ads and trackers at DNS level across browsers, apps, routers, and smart TVs.
home.f3Desc: Control adult content, gambling, gaming, social media, and custom categories by profile or device.
home.f4Desc: Route users to the nearest DNS node using GeoDNS and future-ready Anycast architecture.
home.f5Desc: Understand top domains, blocked requests, devices, categories, nodes, and query trends.
home.f6Desc: Manage members, roles, API keys, shared profiles, and centralized DNS policies for teams.
home.networkDesc: Control plane, DNS agents, CoreDNS nodes, NATS messaging, Redis config cache, and ClickHouse analytics.
home.pricingDesc: Start free, upgrade when you need more profiles, logs, teams, and advanced protection.
home.freeDesc: For personal testing and basic protection.
home.ctaDesc: Configure DoH, DoT, or classic DNS on your phone, laptop, router, browser, or team network.

auth.signInDesc: Admin: use admin · Member: use user
auth.name: Name
auth.passwordMin: Password must be at least 6 characters
auth.highlightPrimary: One account manages every DNS policy

dashboard.title: Member Center
dashboard.subtitle: Manage your DNS profiles, devices, security policies, query logs, and real-time statistics.
dashboard.guideDesc: Configure Secure DNS (DoH) in Google Chrome so the browser uses your OcerDNS Profile for security, privacy, and parental filtering.
dashboard.guideStep1Desc: Type chrome://settings/security into the address bar and press Enter.
dashboard.guideStep2Desc: Scroll down to the "Advanced" section and turn on "Use secure DNS".
dashboard.guideStep3Title: Choose "Custom" and enter your DoH URL
dashboard.guideStep3Desc: Pick "Choose your own service provider" and paste the DoH URL below into the input field.
dashboard.guideStep4Desc: After saving, visit https://1.1.1.1/help to verify that DNS has switched to OcerDNS.
dashboard.guideNote: This setting only applies to the current browser. To cover all devices, configure DNS on your router or operating system.

security.title: Security
security.desc: Advanced security protection to keep your network safe
security.blockPhishingDesc: Block phishing and credential harvesting sites
security.blockCryptojackingDesc: Prevent unauthorized use of your devices for cryptocurrency mining.
security.threatIntelDesc: Use threat intelligence feeds to block known malicious domains.
security.aiDetectionDesc: Use artificial intelligence to detect and block emerging threats.
security.googleSafeBrowsingDesc: Use Google Safe Browsing to block malware and phishing sites. This technology checks billions of links daily and identifies unsafe websites. Unlike the version built into some browsers, this does not associate your IP address with malicious sites and does not allow bypassing the block.
security.dnsRebindDesc: Block DNS query results containing local IP addresses, preventing hackers from manipulating local devices over the internet.
security.idnHomoDesc: Block internationalized domain names that visually resemble legitimate domains.
security.typoSquatDesc: Block domains that are misspellings of popular websites, often used for phishing.
security.dgaDesc: Domains generated by Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) are often used by various malware or viruses as command and control servers.
security.newDomainsDesc: Block domains registered in the last 30 days, as they are often used for malicious purposes.
security.dynamicDnsDesc: Block dynamic DNS services that are commonly used by attackers to maintain access to compromised systems.
security.parkedDomainsDesc: Block domains that are parked and not hosting legitimate content.
security.blockTldDesc: Block entire top-level domains that are commonly associated with malicious activity.
security.childAbuseDesc: Block websites containing child sexual abuse material.

privacy.title: Block Lists
privacy.desc: Get rid of ads and tracking with popular block lists that are refreshed in real-time.
privacy.blockTelemetryDesc: Block telemetry and update check domains
privacy.anonymizeIPDesc: Only store client IP as hash in logs

blocklists.title: Block Lists
blocklists.adsTrackingDesc: Comprehensive coverage to block ads and trackers from almost every country. This is the recommended base block list.
blocklists.deepTrackingDesc: Blocks deep tracking software that typically operates at the operating system level and knows everything you do on your device. This may include all websites you visit, everything you type, or your location.
blocklists.thirdPartyTrackingDesc: Automatically detects and blocks third-party trackers that disguise themselves as first-party to evade privacy protections like ITP.

special.title: Special Settings
special.allowMarketingDesc: Allow marketing or tracking domains commonly found in shopping websites, emails, or search results. These links typically only trigger when manually clicked.

privacy.note: Your IP address will be automatically hidden from these sites to protect your privacy.

parental.title: Parental Control
parental.desc: Restrict access to specific websites, apps and games.
parental.blockAdultDesc: Block pornographic and adult content domains
parental.safeSearchDesc: Enforce safe search on major search engines
parental.blockBypassDesc: Block or hinder methods attempting to bypass blocking. This includes VPNs, proxy software, Tor network and encrypted DNS.
parental.note: Your settings will be automatically applied to all connected devices.

websites.title: Websites, Apps & Games
websites.desc: Restrict access to specific websites, apps and games.
websites.websitesDesc: Block specific website domains, such as Douyin, TikTok, etc.
websites.gamesDesc: Block specific gaming websites and applications.

categories.title: Category Filters
categories.desc: Restrict access to specific categories of websites and apps.
categories.adultDesc: Block adult and pornographic content. This includes prostitution websites, Pornhub and similar sites.

timeLimit.title: Time Limits
timeLimit.desc: Set a time period for one or more days of the week during which selected websites, apps, games and categories can be freely used.

safeSearch.title: Safe Search
safeSearch.desc: Filter out search results containing adult content on major search engines, including images and videos.
safeSearch.safeSearchDesc: Filter out search results containing adult content on major search engines, including images and videos. If a search engine does not support this feature, the entire engine will be blocked.
safeSearch.forceSafeSearchDesc: Force all search engines to use safe search mode, which cannot be turned off by users.
safeSearch.youtubeRestrictedDesc: Filter adult videos on YouTube and block embedded adult videos from being watched on other websites. This will also hide all comments.
safeSearch.forceYoutubeRestrictedDesc: Force YouTube to use restricted mode, which cannot be turned off by users.
safeSearch.blockBypassDesc: Prevent users from bypassing parental controls via proxy or VPN.

parental.name: Name

denylist.title: Denylist
denylist.desc: Custom domains to block. These domains will be denied regardless of other rules.

allowlist.title: Allowlist
allowlist.desc: Custom domains to always allow. Allowlist takes priority over all blocking rules.
allowlist.priorityNote: Allowlist rules take priority over Security, Privacy, Parental Control, and Denylist.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.encryptedDnsDesc: Percentage of queries using DNS-over-HTTPS / DNS-over-TLS
analytics.dnssecDesc: Percentage of queries using DNSSEC validation
analytics.gafamDesc: 「GAFAM」（Google, Amazon, Facebook, Apple, Microsoft）are the five dominant internet companies operating many popular services, also under different names such as Facebook's WhatsApp and Instagram.
